mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the real-time web. It is ideal for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time collaboration. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management in a Kubernetes environment compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the SurrealDB CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for SurrealDB to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/surrealdb/main/deploy/crds/surrealdb.com_surrealdbs.yaml

```

> Ensure CRDs are installed before deploying the application.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --create-namespace --version 3.0.2

```

> Change 'surreal' to your desired namespace.

steps.title: Create an example SurrealDB instance
steps.description:
This step creates an instance of SurrealDB to start using the database.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: my-surrealdb
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

```

> This creates a basic SurrealDB instance.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: my-surrealdb
  namespace: surreal
spec:
  replicas: 1
EOF

steps.title: Verify the SurrealDB instance is ready
steps.description:
This command checks if the SurrealDB instance is running and ready to accept connections.

```bash

kubectl get surrealdb -n surreal

```

> Look for the status 'Running'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes SurrealDB and its associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surreal

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/surrealdb/surrealdb/main/deploy/crds/surrealdb.com_surrealdbs.yaml

```

> This command removes the SurrealDB release.
> This removes the CRDs associated with SurrealDB.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data. This process will upgrade SurrealDB to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl cp surrealdb/surrealdb-0:/data /backup/surrealdb_backup

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surreal --version 3.0.2

kubectl get surrealdb -n surreal

```

> Backup your data before proceeding.
> This command upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verify that the SurrealDB instance is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surreal' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB service is not reachable, possibly due to the pod not being ready.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n surreal' to check the pod status and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB instance has not been created or is in a failed state.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CR was applied correctly and check for errors in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image cannot be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your Helm values or deployment configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for multi-model queries and improved real-time collaboration features.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this release.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 3.0.1, ensure to review the new configuration options for real-time features.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB instance listed with 'Running' status when executing 'kubectl get surrealdb -n surreal'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that Helm is installed.
